merger_description:
BINGO Acquisitions Pty Ltd, trading as BINGO Industries (**BINGO**), proposes to acquire the assets and the business of JLW Services Pty Ltd (**JLW**) (the **Acquisition**).

BINGO is a recycling and waste management company with operations in New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland. BINGO provides collection, processing, recycling and disposal services for building and demolition waste as well as for commercial and industrial waste.

JLW is based in Cootamundra, New South Wales, and provides collection, processing and recycling services for end-of-life tyres and mattresses, as well as on-site shredding and mine site clean-up services, servicing mainly regional locations in New South Wales, Queensland, Victoria and South Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BINGO Acquisitions Pty Ltd, trading as BINGO Industries (Bingo
Industries), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of the assets of JLW Services Pty Ltd (JLW
Services), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Bingo Industries is a recycling and waste management
company with operations in New South Wales, Victoria and
Queensland. Bingo Industries provides collection, processing,
recycling and disposal services for building and demolition waste as
well as for commercial and industrial waste.
The target, JLW Services, is based in Cootamundra, New South
Wales, and provides collection, processing and recycling services for
end-of-life tyres and mattresses, as well as onsite shredding and
mine site clean-up services, servicing mainly regional locations in
New South Wales, Queensland, Victoria and South Australia.
Both parties are active in the supply of collection, processing and
recycling services and alternative fuel sources in New South Wales,
Victoria and Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Bingo Industries
and JLW Services in the supply of collection, processing and
recycling services in specialised waste streams, in particular
tyres and mattresses in New South Wales, Victoria and
Queensland
b. there is a small horizontal overlap between Bingo Industries
and JLW Services in the supply of alternative fuel sources to
customers in New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland
c. there are alternative suppliers of collection, processing and
recycling services in specialised waste streams, in particular
tyres and mattresses in New South Wales, Victoria and
Queensland
d. there are alternative suppliers alternative fuel sources in
New South Wales, Victoria and Queensland.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
